title: Tickets, Assets, CMDB, Knowledge, SLAs, and Support Evidence · Independent Support Trial
summary: A different user, device, environment, symptom, constraint, and changed condition requires an independent transfer of tickets, assets, cmdb, knowledge, slas, and support evidence into a new auditable accessible support record system. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of auditable accessible support record system with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record requester-safe contact, user impact, scope, severity, service, asset, environment, time, symptom, baseline, reproduction, changes, evidence, owner, state, and next action.
Distinguish urgency, impact, severity, priority, response, restore, resolution, escalation, service target, business hours, pause condition, and communication commitment.
Relate physical and logical assets, configuration items, owners, locations, dependencies, versions, relationships, lifecycle, discovery, confidence, and history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Ticket Quality
steps.instruction:
lab case — Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The lab variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Record requester-safe contact, user impact, scope, severity, service, asset, environment, time, symptom, baseline, reproduction, changes, evidence, owner, state, and next action.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Ticket Quality.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for record requester-safe contact, user impact, scope, severity, service, asset, environment, time, symptom, baseline, reproduction, changes, evidence, owner, state, and next action. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Lab Ticket Quality, keep Ticket Quality connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Duplicate tickets, mismatched assets, copied workarounds, breached targets, excess personal data, and weak closure hide the causal record. The lab variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Record requester-safe contact, user impact, scope, severity, service, asset, environment, time, symptom, baseline, reproduction, changes, evidence, owner, state, and next action.

content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Closure Handoff symptom.

stimulus.title: Closure Handoff failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Closing a ticket means the technical symptom stopped once.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Closure Handoff condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Closure Handoff.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend the transferred model · Lab Closure Handoff

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Confirm user outcome, changed case, side effects, monitoring, prevention, asset and knowledge updates, retained uncertainty, privacy minimization, accessible communication, and acceptance.
